title: The Strong Box

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: George delivers what should be a definitive breakup speech
jokes.punchline: Maura simply refuses with a flat 'No' as if breakups are negotiable
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language - one word completely subverts the premise that breakups are unilateral decisions. Sets up the entire episode's absurd logic.

jokes.text: All right.
jokes.setup: George has been told his breakup attempt was rejected
jokes.punchline: His passive acceptance reveals his complete lack of backbone
jokes.explanation: Pure George character moment - his resignation to the absurd situation is both pathetic and hilarious.

jokes.text: It's not like you're launching missiles and you both have to turn your keys.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining that breakups don't require mutual consent
jokes.punchline: The nuclear submarine metaphor for relationship termination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from relationship advice to nuclear warfare metaphor. Creates a callback structure for the episode.

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Mashed Potatoes, you are so good.
jokes.setup: George listing Maura's annoying habits to justify the breakup
jokes.punchline: The specific example of her talking to her food in baby talk
jokes.explanation: Specific and cringe-worthy detail that makes Maura's annoying behavior vivid and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm afraid we're out of time.
jokes.setup: George is about to share intimate bedroom details
jokes.punchline: Jerry cuts him off with a talk show host-style dismissal
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall slightly, treating their conversation like a TV segment.

jokes.text: You already have an in. You have the same first name. Jerry.
jokes.setup: Kramer thinking Jerry Lewis cufflinks will help him connect with Jerry Lewis
jokes.punchline: Jerry points out the obvious shared name connection
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor pointing out Kramer's overthinking of a simple situation.

jokes.text: Oh, that'll intrigue him.
jokes.setup: Jerry sarcastically suggesting the shared name is meaningful
jokes.punchline: Kramer takes the sarcasm literally and agrees
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer missing sarcasm completely, showing his obliviousness to social cues.

jokes.text: George Peppard has been dead for years.
jokes.setup: Kramer claiming his name strategy worked with George Peppard
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact correction about Peppard being deceased
jokes.explanation: Deadpan delivery of factual information that completely undermines Kramer's story.

jokes.text: Well, whoever he was, he knew a lot about The A-Team.
jokes.setup: Kramer has just been told George Peppard is dead
jokes.punchline: He dismisses this detail but validates the person's A-Team knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - death is less important than A-Team expertise. Shows his warped priorities.

jokes.text: Lethal injection, feels like pasta. You know, it's painless, I don't want anything too heavy.
jokes.setup: Discussion of choosing last meal based on execution method
jokes.punchline: Jerry's casual food pairing with lethal injection like it's dinner planning
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treated with casual restaurant logic. The juxtaposition of death and dining etiquette is perfectly Seinfeld.

jokes.text: I think you're very beautiful. That'll do.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking what he can tell her about himself
jokes.punchline: Generic compliment followed by her immediate satisfaction with minimal information
jokes.explanation: Shows Elaine's low standards when attracted to someone - willing to accept non-information as sufficient.

jokes.text: Or he's a crime fighter safeguarding his secret identity.
jokes.setup: Elaine wondering why her date is secretive about personal details
jokes.punchline: Jerry's leap from 'in relationship' to 'superhero' explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry escalation from mundane to ridiculous. Sets up the superhero running gag for the episode.

jokes.text: Elaine, you could be dating the Green Lantern.
jokes.setup: Building on the superhero theory
jokes.punchline: Specific superhero identification makes it more absurd
jokes.explanation: Commits fully to the superhero bit by naming a specific character, making the absurdity more concrete.

jokes.text: I don't care for jewelry on men.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Green Lantern's power ring
jokes.punchline: Elaine dismisses superhero based on fashion preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine priorities - judges potential superhero boyfriend on jewelry preferences rather than world-saving abilities.

jokes.text: You were in the Army? Briefly.
jokes.setup: Jerry surprised to learn Kramer was in the Army
jokes.punchline: Kramer's one-word qualifier suggests very brief service
jokes.explanation: Single word response that implies a story we'll never hear but can imagine was disastrous.

jokes.text: Literally? What's that supposed to mean?
jokes.setup: Kramer says someone finding the key would literally hold the key to his possessions
jokes.punchline: Jerry questions the redundant use of 'literally' in this context
jokes.explanation: Meta-linguistic humor about the misuse of 'literally' - ahead of its time given modern discourse about this word.

jokes.text: How long did you last? Well, that's classified.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Kramer's Army service duration
jokes.punchline: Kramer treating his likely brief/disastrous service as state secrets
jokes.explanation: Kramer's inflated sense of importance about what was probably a very short, unsuccessful military stint.

jokes.text: You peeked. This is your hiding place? It was under a spoon.
jokes.setup: Kramer has been secretive about where he hid the key
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately finds it in the most obvious, unsecure location
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Kramer's security paranoia - his 'secret' hiding place is laughably obvious.

jokes.text: It's like launching missiles from a submarine. Both of us have to turn our keys.
jokes.setup: Maura explaining why she won't accept the breakup
jokes.punchline: She uses Jerry's own nuclear metaphor but gets the details wrong
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure but she gets the military detail wrong, making her seem both stubborn and uninformed.

jokes.text: Turn your key, Maura. Turn your key.
jokes.setup: George trying to force the breakup using the military metaphor
jokes.punchline: His desperate pleading like he's trying to prevent nuclear war
jokes.explanation: George's desperation escalates the absurd metaphor to its logical extreme - treating breakup like nuclear crisis.

jokes.text: Married. That's it, I'm chucking the flower.
jokes.setup: Elaine sees her mystery date with another woman
jokes.punchline: Her immediate romantic disillusionment expressed through flower disposal
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy gesture that shows Elaine's quick romantic abandonment when faced with complications.

jokes.text: You'll chicken out at the last second. Yeah, you're right.
jokes.setup: Jerry warning Elaine won't catch the key he drops
jokes.punchline: Elaine immediately agreeing with his assessment of her cowardice
jokes.explanation: Self-aware character moment where Elaine knows her own limitations and admits them immediately.

jokes.text: Nodding off!
jokes.setup: Jerry discussing how he can't eat big meals before sleep anymore
jokes.punchline: He catches himself falling asleep while hanging out the window
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment - the irony of falling asleep while discussing sleep issues.

jokes.text: It went in the sewer.
jokes.setup: Jerry throws food down to Elaine
jokes.punchline: Despite trying to help, it goes to the worst possible place
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy payoff - predictable but necessary for the scene structure.

jokes.text: You're so obsessed with me.
jokes.setup: Jerry found Kramer's poorly hidden key
jokes.punchline: Kramer turns his bad hiding into Jerry being obsessed with him
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer deflection - turning his incompetence into someone else's character flaw.

jokes.text: Flirted with the menu guy.
jokes.setup: Elaine explaining how she got into the building
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using flirtation for basic services
jokes.explanation: Shows Elaine's pragmatic approach to getting what she wants, using feminine wiles for mundane goals.

jokes.text: Well, he could be a superhero. You should have seen him run.
jokes.setup: Discussing the mystery man's quick escape
jokes.punchline: Jerry maintains the superhero theory based on running speed
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag but without much escalation or fresh perspective.

jokes.text: This is not an Easter egg hunt for your childish amusement.
jokes.setup: Kramer is angry about Jerry finding his key
jokes.punchline: He describes Jerry's behavior as an Easter egg hunt
jokes.explanation: Kramer's inflated indignation expressed through holiday metaphor - treats key-finding as juvenile game.

jokes.text: Sounds like a scam. I'm very sorry.
jokes.setup: Phil explains he lives in the building but forgot his key
jokes.punchline: Jerry's overly polite rejection of his obvious neighbor
jokes.explanation: Jerry's paranoia turning reasonable explanation into elaborate con - polite but absurd suspicion.

jokes.text: That ginger ale at the coffee shop is just Coke and Sprite mixed together. How can I prove it? Damn it, I can't. Damn it.
jokes.setup: George has a theory about fake ginger ale
jokes.punchline: His frustration at being unable to prove his conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect George paranoia - obsessing over unprovable beverage fraud with genuine emotional investment.

jokes.text: Why does it always seem like I'm the only one working at this breakup?
jokes.setup: George frustrated that Maura won't accept their breakup
jokes.punchline: Treating breakup like a collaborative project he's doing alone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant relationship observation - treating breakup like group project where one person does all the work. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Mr. Apple, you have a brown spot.
jokes.setup: Earlier George mentioned Maura talks to her food
jokes.punchline: We see it in action - she addresses an apple by name about its imperfection
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows rather than tells. Makes George's complaint concrete and cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: This is wrong. I should go.
jokes.setup: Elaine is in Glenn's apartment about to get romantic
jokes.punchline: Her sudden moral awakening at the wrong moment
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy beat - conscience kicking in at inconvenient moment. Predictable timing.

jokes.text: Lady, I'm not his wife. I'm his welfare caseworker.
jokes.setup: Elaine assumes the woman is Glenn's wife and apologizes for homewrecking
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Glenn is on welfare, not married
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reframes the entire subplot. Changes from adultery to poverty in one line.

jokes.text: So he's.... Poor.
jokes.setup: Elaine processing the welfare caseworker revelation
jokes.punchline: Her slow realization stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Simple realization beat that emphasizes the anti-climactic nature of the revelation.

jokes.text: Is he wretchedly poor? Does he wear one of those barrels with the straps?
jokes.setup: Jerry learning about Glenn's poverty
jokes.punchline: Escalating to cartoonish Depression-era poverty imagery
jokes.explanation: Jerry's tendency to escalate to absurd extremes - referencing cartoon poverty imagery that's decades out of date.

jokes.text: He probably busted it up and burned it for heat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Glenn wears a poverty barrel
jokes.punchline: Jerry imagining he destroyed it for fuel
jokes.explanation: Continues the Depression-era poverty theme with practical but dark humor about survival priorities.

jokes.text: So when are you giving Boxcar Willie his walking papers?
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Elaine should handle her poor boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Jerry nicknames Glenn after famous hobo performer
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to country performer known for hobo persona - creates memorable nickname for Glenn.

jokes.text: We gave him a few bucks. He goes in the alley across the street.
jokes.setup: Elaine recalling how they handled a homeless man
jokes.punchline: Casually describing paying someone to urinate elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about urban problem-solving - treating human dignity as a minor expense account item.

jokes.text: Apparently, I was unable to break up beyond a reasonable doubt.
jokes.setup: George explaining his continued relationship with Maura
jokes.punchline: Using legal terminology for relationship failure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant legal metaphor for relationship dynamics. Suggests Maura treats breakup like criminal trial requiring proof.

jokes.text: He's invulnerable to creditors.
jokes.setup: Continuing superhero jokes about Glenn's poverty
jokes.punchline: Poverty as superpower - can't lose what you don't have
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on superhero powers - financial immunity through having nothing to lose.

jokes.text: He's the Got-No-Green Lantern.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Green Lantern/poverty mashup
jokes.punchline: Pun combining superhero name with financial state
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works on multiple levels but not particularly innovative within the established superhero bit.

jokes.text: Hey, maybe his girlfriend is Lois Loan.
jokes.setup: Continuing Superman/poverty theme
jokes.punchline: Pun on Lois Lane referencing financial borrowing
jokes.explanation: Clean pun that maintains the superhero poverty theme, though by now the bit is getting extended.

jokes.text: Well crafted.
jokes.setup: Jerry makes the Lois Loan pun
jokes.punchline: George gives critical appreciation for the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the joke itself, showing characters as connoisseurs of wordplay quality.

jokes.text: She's too tan. It's the middle of the winter, she's like a carrot.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking why George never pursued this secretary
jokes.punchline: His shallow complaint about seasonal tanning
jokes.explanation: Classic George shallow reasoning - turning minor cosmetic issue into deal-breaker with vegetable comparison.

jokes.text: He can wipe out his account in a single bounce.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Glenn's financial vulnerability
jokes.punchline: Superhero power description for bounced checks
jokes.explanation: Continues the superhero poverty bit but without much escalation. The theme is running out of steam.

jokes.text: So Phil won't be compulsively looking for it like some people.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining why he hid the key at Phil's without telling him
jokes.punchline: His dig at Jerry for being 'compulsive' about finding hidden things
jokes.explanation: Kramer's continued deflection of his poor hiding skills by blaming Jerry's 'obsessive' behavior.

jokes.text: Are you sure? Because it could really help me out of a jam.
jokes.setup: Secretary wants to wait before sleeping with George
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to use sex to solve his breakup problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect George desperation - trying to make adultery seem like favor rather than mutual desire.

jokes.text: All right, that's it. How do you spell your last name?
jokes.setup: Glenn is eating garbage donuts and calling them rare
jokes.punchline: Elaine decides to write him a check to end the relationship
jokes.explanation: Elaine's breaking point perfectly timed - the garbage donuts are the final straw that makes paying him off reasonable.

jokes.text: You're the bear claw in the garbage bag of my life.
jokes.setup: Glenn has been talking about garbage donuts as treasures
jokes.punchline: His romantic declaration using garbage/treasure metaphor
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poetic romantic declaration that's simultaneously sweet and disgusting. Perfectly encapsulates Glenn's character.

jokes.text: That's my wife.
jokes.setup: Jerry assumes Phil's wife is his maid
jokes.punchline: Phil's correction revealing Jerry's offensive assumption
jokes.explanation: Classic social discomfort moment - Jerry's privileged assumption creates awkward situation with neighbor.

jokes.text: That's why I took them out of your drawer and put them in my strongbox.
jokes.setup: Jerry can't find his cufflinks for the big event
jokes.punchline: Kramer reveals he 'protected' them without permission
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer 'helping' that makes things worse - his good intentions create the problem they're trying to solve.

jokes.text: And who buries a bird?
jokes.setup: Learning about Fredo's pet cemetery burial
jokes.punchline: Jerry's incredulous reaction to elaborate bird funeral
jokes.explanation: Jerry's practical observation about excessive pet mourning - typical Seinfeld questioning of social conventions.

jokes.text: I hid the key in Fredo's food dish. That's a weird coincidence.
jokes.setup: Learning that Fredo was poisoned
jokes.punchline: Kramer casually reveals he's responsible while calling it coincidence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor as Kramer unknowingly confesses to bird murder while treating it as minor coincidence.

jokes.text: Fredo was weak and stupid. He shouldn't have eaten that key.
jokes.setup: Kramer being told he killed the bird
jokes.punchline: He blames the bird for eating the poisonous key
jokes.explanation: Brilliant victim-blaming logic that references The Godfather while showing Kramer's complete moral disconnection. Darkly hilarious.

jokes.text: The actor that played Jesus made some odd choices.
jokes.setup: George returning from staged reading of Godspell
jokes.punchline: His critical review of Jesus's performance
jokes.explanation: Casually reviewing the Son of God's acting choices like any other community theater performer. Perfectly mundane blasphemy.

jokes.text: What we have is too important. We can work through this.
jokes.setup: George's affair has been exposed to both women
jokes.punchline: Maura treating adultery as relationship bump they can overcome
jokes.explanation: Maura's relentless relationship optimism reaches absurd heights - even adultery won't deter her commitment.

jokes.text: I guess I'm Lois Loan.
jokes.setup: Glenn's wife asking who Elaine is
jokes.punchline: Elaine uses Jerry's earlier pun to describe her situation
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier pun that works in context but doesn't add much new comedic value.

jokes.text: Listen, I heard that Lassie number three's buried here. I'm gonna go check it out.
jokes.setup: Kramer and Jerry are in pet cemetery to dig up Fredo
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual interest in celebrity pet graves while grave robbing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer priority confusion - treating grave robbery as opportunity for celebrity pet tourism.

jokes.text: Hey, Kramer. I dug Fredo out. Now let's cut him open.
jokes.setup: Jerry has successfully exhumed the bird
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact announcement about bird autopsy to Phil and wife
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy peak - Jerry's casual announcement of desecration to the grieving pet parents. Perfect timing and delivery.

jokes.text: Give me 40. You'll never see me again.
jokes.setup: Elaine offering various amounts to end their relationship
jokes.punchline: Glenn negotiating his own breakup price
jokes.explanation: Perfect transactional relationship ending - Glenn's practical acceptance of payment for disappearing shows his priorities.

jokes.text: Well, will you look at that. I guess I forgot to lock it.
jokes.setup: After all the security paranoia and grave robbing for the key
jokes.punchline: The strongbox was unlocked the entire time
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending that makes all the elaborate schemes meaningless. Classic Seinfeld futility - all that effort for nothing.

jokes.text: Really one for the books.
jokes.setup: Jerry processing that they committed pet grave robbery for nothing
jokes.punchline: His understated reaction to the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry understatement to close the episode - treating grave desecration as mildly noteworthy rather than horrifying.

tmdb_overview: Jerry keeps finding Kramer's hidden strongbox key; Elaine dates a mysterious man.
